Yes. Recording happens right in the browser, no app install or extension. Members tap, talk, send. The voice note attaches just like any other message.

Yes, that's what it's built for. Midway reads your member list and ties every conversation to a real account. No second login, no second profile.

Yes, as long as your Webflow site uses Memberstack. Midway authenticates through Memberstack, so it runs on any Memberstack-powered Webflow site. Paste one iframe and your members get real-time direct messages and voice notes, signed in with the account they already have.

results.title: Midway Chat: Real-time member chat for Memberstack and Webflow sites
results.content:
Magnaem

Image 15: Midway Chat Midway Chat

Maker

@ansari_adinGreat question. Short version: it isn't one always-on websocket per member. The connection only exists while a member has the inbox open and if they close the tab and it tears down. Most active members open the inbox for a small fraction of their session, so peak concurrent connections are typically a small fraction of total MAU.

Under the hood the realtime layer is managed pub/sub: an open inbox subscribes to two channels, the current conversation and a personal notifications channel, and messages, typing indicators, reactions, and read receipts all ride through those as discrete events rather than continuous streams. Cost scales with message volume and peak concurrents, not with how many "active" members exist on paper. [...] , Circle (another login), or wiring up a chat API like TalkJS. Midway is one iframe embed. Paste it into Webflow, point it at your Memberstack project, and your members get DMs with voice notes, replies, reactions, typing indicators, read receipts, online presence, and chat-request gating so nobody gets spammed. There are other ways to put chat on a site (TalkJS, Stream, Memberchat in the Memberstack ecosystem). Midway's tradeoff is being built specifically around Memberstack auth and Webflow embedding, so the setup is minutes instead of days, and your site stays the home of the community instead of pointing people to a third-party app. Free plan to try, paid plans when you scale past a couple hundred MAU.

I run community launches, and the Memberstack-native part is the real unlock — chat that inherits the membership instead of living in a separate Discord with its own login and lost branding. The edge case I'd test first: when someone is downgraded or removed in Memberstack, does Midway revoke their chat access and DM ability in real time, or is there a sync lag where a churned member can still message people? And do their earlier DMs stay visible to the other party once they lose access?

results.title: How to Add Member Chat to Your Memberstack + Webflow Site (Midway Setup)
results.content:
# How to Add Member Chat to Your Memberstack + Webflow Site (Midway Setup)
## Midway Chat 

### Description
1 views
Posted: 29 Jun 2026
In this video I walk through what Midway is, who it's for, and how to get started in a few minutes. 

Midway is an embeddable real-time chat layer for Memberstack sites built on Webflow, adding DMs, group chats, voice notes, files, replies, reactions, online presence, typing indicators, and read receipts to any members area with no backend to maintain. 

Try Midway free: midwaychat.app [...] Hey, I'm Mcn the creator of Midway and I just wanted to give you a quick walkthrough of what Midway is all about. Okay, so Midway is a dropin member chat for member stack powered websites and it simply adds realtime direct messages, voice notes, um replies and reactions, typing indicators and red receipts through a single iframe inbed on your Web Flow website. and it doesn't require any back-end configurations on your part and members can simply just log in with their existing member stack account. Okay. So once you are on the homepage you are then going to create a new account. So you can do that by clicking on the embed free button over here and that would lead you to the sign up page. So I'm already signed up and I have been redirected to my dashboard. So once you have created an [...] requests, as well as the members view. And from members, I can see all of the members that are currently inside of um the admins member stack app. So the entire member community. And I can simply click on one to message um to send a request and message or click on any others as well. Okay, so in the next video I'll walk you through how real-time communication between members actually works. And yeah, I'll see you in the next one. Bye.

results.title: Drop-in member chat for Memberstack + Webflow sites
results.content:
site profile thumbnail

Open live site

Member chat for Memberstack + Webflow sites. One iframe, native to your auth, with DMs, group chats, voice notes, replies, and reactions out of the box. Built for Webflow agencies and course creators who'd rather ship today than wire an SDK or send members to Discord. One iframe. Paste it on a page, gate it with Memberstack like any other element, you're done. No SDK, no identity bridge to maintain and it reads from Memberstack directly. Add a chat to your website that includes voice notes, replies and reactions which are in the default plan, not bolt-ons. If your members already use them in iMessage and Slack, they expect them here. Sign up here:
